pi_items.source: File exists at C:\workspace\data\0001.vital (21,063,820 bytes). Confirmed as VitalDB case 1 from PhysioNet repository (https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/). Loaded and parsed successfully with vitaldb Python library.

Loaded 0001.vital with vitaldb library: vf.to_numpy(['Solar8000/HR'], interval=1) returned 11,543 rows (0-indexed = 11,542 seconds). 11542s = 3*3600 + 12*60 + 22 = 11542. Also confirmed visually in Vital Recorder title bar during live testing.
vitaldb library: to_numpy returned 11,543 rows at 1s interval. Total seconds = rows - 1 = 11,542. Cross-validated: 3*3600 + 12*60 + 22 = 11,542.
Confirmed in Vital Recorder GUI event panel during live testing: 'Surgery started' event at 2100-01-01 00:27:48. Verified via visual_grounding MCP tool on screenshot. Math: 27*60 + 48 = 1668s.
00:27:48 = 27*60 + 48 = 1668 seconds. HR value at t=1668 in vitaldb data = 72.0 bpm (non-NaN, confirming valid data at surgery start). Event panel confirmed in live GUI.
Confirmed in Vital Recorder GUI event panel during live testing: 'Surgery finished' event at 2100-01-01 02:52:48. Verified via visual_grounding MCP tool on screenshot.
02:52:48 = 2*3600 + 52*60 + 48 = 7200 + 3120 + 48 = 10368 seconds. Independently computed and cross-verified.
Intraop = surgery_finished - surgery_started = 10368 - 1668 = 8700 seconds = 145.0 minutes exactly.
10368 - 1668 = 8700 seconds. Cross-validated: 145 minutes * 60 = 8700.
Midpoint = surgery_started + intraop_duration/2 = 1668 + 8700/2 = 1668 + 4350 = 6018 seconds.

vitaldb library: vf.get_track_names() returned 79 tracks total. Grouped by device: BIS=8, SNUADC=4, Primus=32, Solar8000=34, EVENT=1. Total excl EVENT = 8+4+32+34 = 78. Confirmed during live testing via SSH into VM.
vitaldb library: SNUADC tracks = [ART, ECG_II, ECG_V5, PLETH]. Count = 4. These are high-resolution waveform tracks (500 Hz).

vitaldb library: Solar8000 tracks enumerated = ART_DBP, ART_MBP, ART_SBP, BT, ETCO2, FEO2, FIO2, GAS2_AGENT, GAS2_EXPIRED, GAS2_INSPIRED, HR, INCO2, NIBP_DBP, NIBP_MBP, NIBP_SBP, PLETH_HR, PLETH_SPO2, RR_CO2, ST_AVF, ST_AVL, ST_AVR, ST_I, ST_II, ST_III, ST_V5, VENT_INSP_TM, VENT_MAWP, VENT_MV, VENT_PIP, VENT_PPLAT, VENT_RR, VENT_SET_PCP, VENT_SET_TV, VENT_TV. Count = 34.

vitaldb library: Primus tracks enumerated = AWP, CO2, COMPLIANCE, ETCO2, EXP_DES, EXP_SEVO, FEN2O, FEO2, FIN2O, FIO2, INCO2, INSP_DES, INSP_SEVO, MAC, MAWP_MBAR, MV, PAMB_MBAR, PEEP_MBAR, PIP_MBAR, PPLAT_MBAR, RR_CO2, SET_AGE, SET_FIO2, SET_FRESH_FLOW, SET_INSP_PAUSE, SET_INSP_TM, SET_INTER_PEEP, SET_PIP, SET_RR_IPPV, SET_TV_L, TV, VENT_LEAK. Count = 32.
vitaldb library: BIS tracks = [BIS, EEG1_WAV, EEG2_WAV, EMG, SEF, SQI, SR, TOTPOW]. Count = 8.

All three tracks confirmed present in 0001.vital via vitaldb library. Data availability: Solar8000/HR has 5,455 non-NaN values (47.3% coverage), Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2 has 5,496 non-NaN values (47.6%), Primus/ETCO2 has 1,715 non-NaN values (14.9%). All contain clinically meaningful physiological data.
All four waveform tracks confirmed in vitaldb library track list. Visually confirmed in Vital Recorder GUI during live testing: ART (red), ECG_II (green), ECG_V5 (green), PLETH (blue).

Confirmed: SNUADC/ART waveform track present (invasive arterial pressure waveform). Solar8000/ART_SBP has 5,348 non-NaN values (46.3% coverage), Solar8000/ART_DBP and Solar8000/ART_MBP also present. Visually confirmed ART waveform in red in Vital Recorder GUI.
Confirmed: Primus/INSP_SEVO has 1,452 non-NaN values (12.6% coverage) and Primus/EXP_SEVO has 1,452 non-NaN values (12.6% coverage). Both sevoflurane concentration tracks present with real data.

vitaldb library: to_numpy at 1s interval returned 11,543 rows (indices 0 through 11,542). At 1-second intervals, this represents 11,542 seconds of data. CSV export would have 11,542 data rows plus 1 header row.
Verifier parameter. Expected intraop fraction = 8700/11542 = 0.7538. Min bound of 0.40 provides generous tolerance for partial exports or different sampling rates. Reasonable.
Verifier parameter. Max bound of 0.95 ensures the export is a segment, not the full recording (fraction ~0.75 expected). Reasonable.

Standard clinical monitoring ranges per ASA (American Society of Anesthesiologists) guidelines. HR: normal adult range 60-100, alarm range typically 40-160. SpO2: clinically meaningful range 85-100% (below 85 is severe hypoxemia). ETCO2: normal 35-45 mmHg, display range 0-60 covers hypo/hypercapnia.

pi_items.metadata_value: VitalDB open dataset (Seoul National University Hospital)
pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB - open dataset of 6,388 noncardiac surgical cases from Seoul National University Hospital, collected Aug 2016 - Jun 2017, published in Nature Scientific Data (2022)
pi_items.source: Nature Scientific Data publication (doi:10.1038/s41597-022-01411-5, PMC9178032). PhysioNet repository at https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/. Web search confirmed VitalDB is a real, publicly available dataset.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses VitalDB Case 1 (0001.vital), a real surgical recording from Seoul National University Hospital's open perioperative dataset. The recording is 3 hours 12 minutes 22 seconds long (11,542 seconds, 11,543 rows at 1s intervals). Four event markers exist: 'Case started' at 00:00:00, 'Surgery started' at 00:27:48 (1,668s), 'Surgery finished' at 02:52:48 (10,368s), and 'Case finished' at 03:12:22 (11,542s). The intraoperative segment spans 8,700 seconds (145 minutes). The file contains 78 physiological tracks across 4 devices: SNUADC (4 waveform tracks: ART, ECG_II, ECG_V5, PLETH), Solar8000 (34 numeric tracks including HR, ART_SBP, ART_DBP, ART_MBP, PLETH_SPO2, PLETH_HR, ETCO2, BT), Primus (32 tracks including CO2, AWP, INSP_SEVO, EXP_SEVO, ETCO2, MAC, COMPLIANCE), and BIS (8 tracks including BIS, SQI, EEG1_WAV, EEG2_WAV). Invasive arterial blood pressure monitoring IS present (SNUADC/ART waveform + Solar8000 ART_SBP/DBP/MBP numerics). Volatile anesthetic (sevoflurane) monitoring IS present (Primus/INSP_SEVO and Primus/EXP_SEVO). The three parameter tracks to add are Solar8000/HR (5,455 non-NaN values, 47.3% coverage), Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2 (5,496 values, 47.6%), and Primus/ETCO2 (1,715 values, 14.9%). Default waveform display shows ART (red), ECG_II (green), ECG_V5 (green), PLETH (blue), CO2 (yellow), AWP (white). The expected CSV export fraction for the intraoperative segment is approximately 0.75 of total rows.
